python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('file_path', data.get('path', ''))
    if path.endswith('.py'):
        import subprocess
        subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'ruff', 'check', '--fix', '--quiet', path], capture_output=True)
        subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'ruff', 'format', '--quiet', path], capture_output=True)
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, subprocess
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('file_path', data.get('path', ''))
    if path.endswith('.py') and path.startswith('src/') and not path.startswith('src/logging'):
        test_path = path.replace('src/', 'tests/test_', 1)
        import os
        if os.path.isfile(test_path):
            result = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'pytest', test_path, '-x', '-q', '--tb=short'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
            if result.stdout.strip():
                # Only show last 10 lines to avoid context bloat
                lines = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')[-10:]
                print(json.dumps({'message': '\n'.join(lines)}))
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, subprocess
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('file_path', data.get('path', ''))
    if path.endswith('pyproject.toml'):
        result = subprocess.run(['uv', 'sync'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60)
        if result.stdout.strip():
            lines = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')[-5:]
            print(json.dumps({'message': 'Auto uv sync: ' + '; '.join(lines)}))
except: pass
"

bash -c '[ "$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)" != "main" ] || { echo "{\"message\": \"BLOCKED: Cannot edit files on main branch. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feat/description\"}"; exit 2; }'

python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    cmd = data.get('command', '')
    # Block pip
    import re
    if re.match(r'^(pip3?|python3? -m pip)\s', cmd):
        print(json.dumps({'message': 'BLOCKED: Use uv instead of pip. Add: uv add <pkg>. Install: uv sync.'}))
        sys.exit(2)
    # Block destructive
    dangerous = ['rm ', 'DROP TABLE', 'DROP DATABASE', 'TRUNCATE',
                 'git push --force', 'git push -f ', 'git reset --hard', 'git checkout .', 'git restore .',
                 'kubectl delete', 'docker rm -f', ':(){ :|:& };:', '> /dev/sda']
    for d in dangerous:
        if d.lower() in cmd.lower():
            print(json.dumps({'message': f'BLOCKED: Destructive command detected: {d}. Use a safer alternative or confirm with the user.'}))
            sys.exit(2)
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, re
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    cmd = data.get('command', '')
    if re.search(r'\b(DELETE\s+FROM|UPDATE\s+\w+\s+SET)\b', cmd, re.I):
        match = re.search(r'(?:DELETE\s+FROM|UPDATE)\s+(\w+)', cmd, re.I)
        table = match.group(1) if match else 'unknown'
        has_where = bool(re.search(r'\bWHERE\b', cmd, re.I))
        msg = f'DB SAFETY: This will affect table \"{table}\"'
        if not has_where:
            msg += ' with NO WHERE clause (ALL rows!)'
        msg += '. Please confirm with the user before proceeding.'
        print(json.dumps({'message': msg}))
        sys.exit(2)
    orm_dangers = [
        (r'\.delete\s*\(', '.delete()'),
        (r'\.truncate\s*\(', '.truncate()'),
        (r'\bexecute\s*\(.*\b(?:delete|update|truncate|drop)\b', 'execute(delete/update/truncate/drop)'),
        (r'\b(?:remove_all|delete_many|drop_collection|delete_one|delete_all)\s*\(', 'bulk delete operation'),
    ]
    for pattern, name in orm_dangers:
        if re.search(pattern, cmd, re.I):
            print(json.dumps({'message': f'DB SAFETY: Command contains {name} — this could modify or destroy database data. Confirm with the user before proceeding.'}))
            sys.exit(2)
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, re
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('file_path', data.get('path', ''))
    content = data.get('content', '') or data.get('new_string', '')
    if not content or not path:
        sys.exit(0)
    if not path.endswith(('.py', '.sql', '.sh', '.bash', '.js', '.ts', '.tsx')):
        sys.exit(0)
    db_dangers = [
        (r'\bDELETE\s+FROM\b', 'DELETE FROM'),
        (r'\bUPDATE\s+\w+\s+SET\b', 'UPDATE...SET'),
        (r'\bTRUNCATE\b', 'TRUNCATE'),
        (r'\bDROP\s+TABLE\b', 'DROP TABLE'),
        (r'\bDROP\s+DATABASE\b', 'DROP DATABASE'),
        (r'\.delete\s*\(', '.delete()'),
        (r'\.truncate\s*\(', '.truncate()'),
        (r'\bexecute\b.*\b(?:delete|update|truncate|drop)\b', 'execute(delete/update/truncate/drop)'),
    ]
    for pattern, name in db_dangers:
        if re.search(pattern, content, re.I):
            print(json.dumps({'message': f'DB SAFETY: File {path} contains {name} operation. This could modify or destroy database data. Confirm with the user before writing this file.'}))
            sys.exit(2)
except:
    pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, os, subprocess
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    path = data.get('file_path', data.get('path', ''))
    if not path:
        sys.exit(0)
    root = subprocess.run(['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()
    freeze_file = os.path.join(root, '.claude', '.freeze-state') if root else ''
    if not freeze_file or not os.path.isfile(freeze_file):
        sys.exit(0)
    with open(freeze_file) as f:
        frozen_dir = f.read().strip()
    abs_path = os.path.abspath(path)
    if not abs_path.startswith(frozen_dir):
        print(json.dumps({'message': f'BLOCKED: Edit outside frozen directory. Edits restricted to {frozen_dir}. Use /unfreeze to remove restriction.'}))
        sys.exit(2)
except:
    pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, subprocess, re
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    cmd = data.get('command', '')
    if not re.match(r'^git\s+commit', cmd):
        sys.exit(0)
    # SECRET SCANNER: check staged files for hardcoded secrets
    staged = subprocess.run(['git', 'diff', '--cached', '--name-only'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    py_files = [f for f in staged.stdout.strip().split('\n') if f.endswith('.py') and f]
    if not py_files:
        sys.exit(0)
    patterns = [
        r'(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,})',
        r'(api[_-]?key\s*=\s*[\x27\"][^\x27\"]{10,})',
        r'(password\s*=\s*[\x27\"][^\x27\"]{5,})',
        r'(OPENAI_API_KEY\s*=\s*[\x27\"][^\x27\"]+)',
        r'(ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\s*=\s*[\x27\"][^\x27\"]+)',
        r'(Bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9_\-\.]{20,})',
    ]
    import re as regex_mod
    for f in py_files:
        try:
            content = open(f).read()
            for pat in patterns:
                match = regex_mod.search(pat, content)
                if match:
                    print(json.dumps({'message': f'SECRET DETECTED in {f}: possible hardcoded credential. Use pydantic-settings + .env instead.'}))
                    sys.exit(2)
        except: pass
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import sys, json, subprocess, re, os
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    cmd = data.get('command', '')
    if not re.match(r'^git\s+commit', cmd):
        sys.exit(0)
    # MIGRATION REVERSIBILITY TEST: if migrations are staged, test up/down/up
    staged = subprocess.run(['git', 'diff', '--cached', '--name-only'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    has_migration = any('migration' in f or 'alembic/versions' in f for f in staged.stdout.strip().split('\n') if f)
    if not has_migration:
        sys.exit(0)
    if not os.path.isfile('alembic.ini'):
        sys.exit(0)
    # Test: upgrade → downgrade → upgrade
    up1 = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'alembic', 'upgrade', 'head'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
    if up1.returncode != 0:
        print(json.dumps({'message': f'MIGRATION TEST FAILED (upgrade): {up1.stderr[:200]}'}))
        sys.exit(2)
    down = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'alembic', 'downgrade', '-1'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
    if down.returncode != 0:
        print(json.dumps({'message': f'MIGRATION TEST FAILED (downgrade): {down.stderr[:200]}. Your migration cannot be rolled back!'}))
        sys.exit(2)
    up2 = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'alembic', 'upgrade', 'head'], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
    if up2.returncode != 0:
        print(json.dumps({'message': f'MIGRATION TEST FAILED (re-upgrade): {up2.stderr[:200]}'}))
        sys.exit(2)
    print(json.dumps({'message': 'Migration reversibility test: PASSED (upgrade → downgrade → upgrade)'}))
except Exception as e:
    # Don't block commit if test infra isn't set up yet
    pass
"

python3 -c "
import json, subprocess, os
try:
    state = {}
    # Save current branch
    branch = subprocess.run(['git', 'branch', '--show-current'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    state['branch'] = branch.stdout.strip()
    # Save recent git activity
    log = subprocess.run(['git', 'log', '--oneline', '-5'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    state['recent_commits'] = log.stdout.strip()
    # Save modified files
    diff = subprocess.run(['git', 'diff', '--name-only'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    state['modified_files'] = diff.stdout.strip()
    # Save current task context if context.md exists
    if os.path.isfile('docs/context.md'):
        with open('docs/context.md') as f:
            state['context_summary'] = f.read()[:500]
    # Write state file for PostCompact to read
    os.makedirs('.claude', exist_ok=True)
    with open('.claude/pre-compact-state.json', 'w') as f:
        json.dump(state, f)
    print(json.dumps({'message': 'Pre-compact state saved.'}))
except: pass
"

python3 -c "
import json, os
try:
    state_file = '.claude/pre-compact-state.json'
    if os.path.isfile(state_file):
        with open(state_file) as f:
            state = json.load(f)
        msg = 'CONTEXT RESTORED after compaction:\n'
        msg += f'Branch: {state.get("branch", "unknown")}\n'
        msg += f'Recent commits: {state.get("recent_commits", "none")}\n'
        msg += f'Modified files: {state.get("modified_files", "none")}\n'
        if state.get('context_summary'):
            msg += f'Context: {state["context_summary"][:200]}...\n'
        msg += 'RULES: uv not pip | Log LLM calls | Prompts in files | Docstrings required | Close async clients | No fire-and-forget | DB safety'
        print(json.dumps({'message': msg}))
        os.remove(state_file)
except: pass
"

echo '{"message": "POST-COMPACTION REMINDER: 1) uv not pip, 2) Log ALL LLM calls (logs/llm/), 3) Prompts in prompts/ not inline, 4) Module+function docstrings on every .py, 5) Close async clients in lifespan, 6) No fire-and-forget tasks, 7) NEVER delete/update DB without showing count and getting permission."}'
